title: Obamacare Enrollment Drops Sharply as Costs Rise
summary: Americans can’t afford the higher health insurance premiums that resulted from Congress’s refusal to extend federal tax credits.

title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
summary: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.

title: As Israel Entrenches, Frustration With Hezbollah Turns to Support
summary: With the cease-fire fraying and Israel demolishing villages in the south, many Hezbollah supporters in Lebanon are putting aside their annoyance with the group and turning to it for protection.

title: Hezbollah Using Fiber-Optic Drones Against Israeli Targets
summary: The Lebanese militant group is attacking Israeli troops with explosive drones controlled by fiber-optic cables, like those commonly used in the war in Ukraine.

title: Billionaire Chris Larsen Plans to Spend $3.5 Million in NY House Race Amid Midterm Clash Over A.I.
summary: Chris Larsen, who hails from California, plans to spend $3.5 million to help Alex Bores, a New York congressional candidate at the center of a proxy war over A.I. regulation.

title: Republicans Want Tennessee’s Last Democratic House District
summary: Republicans sliced Nashville into three G.O.P.-leaning congressional districts in 2022. After the Supreme Court decision on voting rights, Memphis could be next.

title: Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling Could Fuel New Era of Redistricting Wars
summary: The expected flood of new congressional maps is likely to produce fewer competitive districts, fewer ways for voters to hold elected officials accountable and more polarized politics.

title: What to Know About the Maine Senate Race After Janet Mills Drops Out
summary: The exit of Gov. Janet Mills kicked off the general election early in what is likely to be one of the most important, expensive and combative Senate races of 2026.

title: Nile Rodgers Explains How He Wrote ‘We Are Family,’ ‘Le Freak’ and More
summary: The co-founder of Chic, the de facto house band of New York’s late-70s disco boom, talks about taking inspiration from the city’s club scene.

title: How Taylor Swift Writes a Song, In Her Own Words
summary: The artist shares stories behind some of her biggest hits, her love of a “rant bridge” and how life in the public eye informs the stories she tells in her songs.

title: What the End of Saudi Arabia’s Big-Spending Era Means for the Kingdom and Beyond
summary: The kingdom is pulling back from a costly and high-profile golf venture amid mounting financial concerns, raising questions about its plans.

title: Comey Indictment Shows Justice Dept. Got the Message From Bondi’s Firing
summary: In naming only an interim successor as acting attorney general, President Trump has established even greater incentives to execute his most extreme demands, current and former officials say.

title: Jared Kushner, Steve Witkoff and the Profitable Business of Peace
summary: The Trump administration is turning diplomacy into an asset to be leveraged.

title: The World’s Central Banks Are Wrestling With a Gigantic Problem
summary: Inflationary surges and economic slowdowns set off by the war in Iran, tariffs and other Trump policies have forced monetary officials to adjust, our columnist says.

title: Why Countries Are Stocking Up on Gold
summary: As risk has escalated, central banks have bought more gold to stash in reserve. A widening Middle East war could add to the urgency.

title: Where in the World Is All That Gold Stored?
summary: As central banks buy more gold, where to put all that heavy metal is an increasingly important question. Reserves must be secure and ready to trade in a crisis.

title: Fed Officials Cite Inflation Concerns in Defending Dissents
summary: The Federal Reserve’s meeting this week was the most divisive in decades, a sign that President Trump’s pick to lead the central bank will face opposition if he pursues substantially lower interest rates.

title: Something Liberals and Conservatives Agree On: Hatred of Data Centers
summary: Americans have soured on the projects, polls show, and the sentiment is profoundly bipartisan. How that will change our politics?

title: Top Psychiatrists Call for a Greater Focus on Ceasing Medication
summary: With Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. aiming to rein in the use of psychiatric drugs, psychiatrists are preemptively addressing how and when patients should quit taking them.

title: Paraguay Says It’s Not Abandoning Taiwan. China Has Other Plans.
summary: Beijing is trying to unravel an unlikely long-distance relationship that has endured for decades. Could Paraguay be tempted to stray?

title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Possible Suicide Note Hidden From Public View
summary: An inmate said he discovered the note after Mr. Epstein was found injured in his jail cell, weeks before his death. It’s now locked in a courthouse.

title: How Indigenous Acknowledgments Became a Target in Australia
summary: Recognition of Aboriginal Australians has become standard at most public events. Increasingly, the right is taking aim at the practice.

title: What to Know About ‘Welcome to Country’ Ceremony Used by Indigenous Australians
summary: The tradition, dating back thousands of years, is used by Indigenous Australians to greet visitors to their ancestral lands.

title: Violence Erupts in Australian Town After Arrest of Suspect in Girl’s Killing
summary: Police in Alice Springs fired rubber bullets and tear gas to disperse an angry crowd demanding justice.

title: As Kentucky Derby Becomes a Bigger Business, Locals Feel Left Behind
summary: Locals say rising ticket prices and a late start time for the Oaks race on Friday are hurting residents and restaurants. “The only thing I see is greed,” one steakhouse owner said.

title: In a Small Iowa Town, a Solution to a National Crisis
summary: As the Trump administration guts weather agencies, a state’s low-cost flood tracking system offers a model for responding to deadly inundations.

title: Don’t Underestimate the Fury of Democratic Voters
summary: A Democratic vision of the Tea Party is emerging.

title: The Voting Rights Act
summary: We explain the significance of the Supreme Court’s decision.

title: Seth Meyers Gives Trump an Earful About Ear Jokes
summary: The “Late Night” host expressed secondhand embarrassment after the president referred to the “beautiful ears” and “super hearing” of a NASA official.

title: Lithuania Says It Broke Up Russian Sabotage and Murder Plots
summary: The arrests of nine people are reminders of the threat Russia poses in Europe at a time when Washington has shifted focus to the Middle East.

title: In Bulgaria, a New Government Challenges an Old Puppet Master
summary: Delyan Peevski was a focus of protests that led to Bulgarian elections. Can a new government shake his hold on power?

title: Why So Many People Already Own Shares of Elon Musk’s SpaceX
summary: Even before the rocket company holds a major initial public offering, many people own stock in it through so-called special purpose vehicles.

title: What We Know About New York’s Proposed Tax Increases
summary: City leaders and state lawmakers are pushing Gov. Kathy Hochul to raise taxes on the wealthy, and they have floated several options.
